hero_subtitle: SFTY helps compliance teams consolidate alerts, provider outputs and case data into audit-ready evidence packages for consistent and defensible suspicious transaction reporting.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Evidence becomes harder to reconstruct and defend
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to assemble consistent, audit-ready STR evidence when provider outputs and case materials remain dispersed across multiple systems. This fragmentation slows suspicious transaction drafting and weakens traceability between alerts, decisions, and regulatory reporting requirements.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY aggregates alerts, provider results, case reviews and decision logs into a unified evidence package so compliance teams can prepare traceable and audit-ready STR documentation efficiently.
workflow_title: From alerts to SAR evidence packages
workflow_description: SFTY organizes alerts, provider results and case reviews into structured evidence packages that support defensible suspicious transaction report drafting workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY transforms scattered alert outputs into structured cases, preserving audit-ready evidence and decisions to support clear, defensible STR reporting workflows for compliance teams.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASP compliance teams creating structured, audit-ready STR evidence packages with controlled provider orchestration and enterprise deployment governance.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how to prepare STR evidence packages

ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how STR drafts connect to regulatory reporting requirements
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how provider outputs integrate into suspicious activity cases
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides compliance teams with a connected layer to manage AML operations, connecting alerts, cases, regulatory rules, and reporting workflows for structured, auditable evidence packages.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support SAR and STR drafting?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures provider data and case details into draft SAR or STR reports, enabling compliance teams to review and finalize evidence packages within an integrated workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs, policy updates and supporting evidence to support audit-ready records for regulatory reviews and internal compliance oversight.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates outputs from KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, consolidating data into one workflow without disrupting existing tool configurations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC or KYT providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing KYC, KYT and other providers, turning their outputs into structured workflows without replacing underlying services.
final_description: Review how SFTY structures alerts, provider data and case evidence into comprehensive suspicious transaction report support.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
